rulings.Case Title: FORD MOTOR CREDIT COMPANY LLC DBA JAGUAR CREDIT VS. MELANIE M. SHELBY et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 16, 2015, Line 12, PLAINTIFF FORD MOTOR CREDIT COMPANY LLC DBA JAGUAR CREDIT'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. Granted in part. Defendant has established that $2,990.71 of her monthly earnings are necessary for her support and are therefore exempt from garnishment. (See CCP sec. 706.051.) The remainder of Defendant's monthly salary, or $238.04, is not exempt. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MARK EMMETT WILSON VS. MIGUEL ALEJANDRO RESCALVO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Reconsideration Based On Law
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 16, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF MARK WILSON'S Motion For Reconsideration Based On Law. The court grants reconsideration but adopts the same ruling granting the motion for summary judgment. Even if the court assumes that Pacific Corporate constitutes new law within the meaning of CCP 1008, the outcome of the summary judgment remains the same. First, the offer in this case clearly sets forth a particular mode of acceptance while the offer in Pacific Corporate did not. In addition, even if plaintiff's offer did not provide for an exclusive manner for acceptance, the undisputed evidence demonstrates that plaintiff did not sufficiently manifest his assent as a matter of law. The undisputed evidence shows that plaintiff signed the offer and unreasonably kept it at his desk rather than return it to defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BRASIL SF, LLC, DBA BOSSA NOVA VS. AINA ENTERPRISES, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendants Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Written Discovery And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday March 16, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT AINA ENTERPRISES, LLC'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Written Discovery And Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion GRANTED. No opposition was filed. Request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $600.00, payable within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DIANA DAIHUA ZHOU VS. NORTHERN CALIFORNIA PRESBYTERIAN HOMES AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 16, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT NORTHERN CALIFORNIA PRESBYTERIAN HOMES AND SERVICES' Motion for Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. The court grants the motion for summary judgment. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that plaintiff cannot establish any of her causes of action. Defendant has shown that it took reasonable steps to prevent discrimination/harassment and conducted a good faith and reasonable investigation of plaintiff's claims of such misconduct. Defendant demonstrates a legitimate business reason for the adverse employment actions and plaintiff fails to present substantial responsive evidence of pretext. The negligent hiring claim fails because there is no evidence that defendant should have known that Ms. Spencer was an unfit employee. The declaratory relief cause of action cannot be established because that remedy is supposed to act prospectively, plaintiff's remedy is to pursue the other causes of action, and the claim is not necessary and proper in this case. See CCP 1061. The court notes that plaintiff's opposing separate statement purports to dispute certain facts, but the document does not include citations to the evidentiary record. The document is defective under CCP 437c(b)(3) and CRC 3.1350(h). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VERA CORT VS. CARL HILSZ **CONVERTED FROM U/D TO LIMITED CIVIL ACTION PER COURT ORDER SEP-29-11**
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production And For A Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday March 16, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF VERA CORT Amended Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production And For A Monetary Sanction. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion GRANTED. Despite the defendant's assertion to the contrary, the evidence shows the document request was served on the proper address by FedEx on October 30, 2104. Thus, the defendant's responses were untimely and all objections are waived. The defendant failed to seek relief from waiver. The defendant also failed to meet and confer. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $1,460, payable within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
